narrative.headline: 2022 Nissan Rogue: engine complaints dominate the owner reports
narrative.summary: Owners filed 237 complaints with NHTSA. Engine is by far the most reported area at 51%, with engine and engine cooling (18%) and the fuel/propulsion system (17%) close behind. No fires, injuries, or deaths are reported in any area. The power train draws the only crashes (2), so it is worth a close look on a test drive.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint area by far: 121 reports (51%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. A common theme is a strong fuel smell in the cabin. Check for any gas odor while driving.

issues.sample: The contact owns a 2022 Nissan Rogue. The contact stated that upon approaching the vehicle, he observed a fluid leak underneath the engine compartment. No warning lights were illuminated. The vehicle was taken to the dealer, who confirmed the fluid leak from an unknown location a
issues.title: Engine And Engine Cooling
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 42 reports (18%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Reports include fluid leaks under the engine. Look underneath for any leaks.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 41 reports (17%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Like the engine complaints, owners describe a gasoline smell inside and outside the car. Sniff for fuel odor during a test drive.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 32 reports (14%) and the only area tied to crashes (2), with no fires or injuries. One report describes the car continuing to accelerate after releasing the pedal. Pay attention to how the car responds to the accelerator.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The fewest complaints at 18 reports (8%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Reports mention the heat going out and weak airflow from the vents. A smaller but recurring area to check.
